meta.title: Historical Figures
meta.subtitle: People who shaped the historical record — searchable.

glossary.heading: Roles — what each figure is chiefly known for

terms.Ruler: Monarchs, presidents, and heads of state.
terms.Explorer: Navigators and pioneers of unknown territory.
terms.Religious: Founders, reformers, and leaders of faith.
terms.Reformer: Activists and figures who drove social change.

meta.footer: A placeholder topic seeded with sample figures. Dates are scholarly estimates where exact dates are unknown.

entries.name: Confucius
entries.desc: Chinese teacher and philosopher whose ideas on ethics and governance shaped East Asian thought.

entries.name: Julius Caesar
entries.desc: Roman general and statesman whose rise ended the Republic and led to the Empire.

entries.name: Cleopatra VII
entries.desc: Last active ruler of Ptolemaic Egypt, allied with Julius Caesar and Mark Antony.

entries.name: Charlemagne
entries.desc: King of the Franks who united much of western Europe and was crowned Emperor in 800.

entries.name: Saladin
entries.desc: First sultan of Egypt and Syria who led Muslim forces during the Crusades.

entries.name: Joan of Arc
entries.desc: French peasant who led armies during the Hundred Years' War and was later canonised.

entries.name: Leonardo da Vinci
entries.desc: Italian polymath of the Renaissance, painter of the Mona Lisa and prolific inventor.

entries.name: Ferdinand Magellan
entries.desc: Portuguese explorer who organised the first expedition to circumnavigate the globe.

entries.name: Martin Luther
entries.desc: German theologian whose Ninety-five Theses sparked the Protestant Reformation.

entries.name: Isaac Newton
entries.desc: English physicist and mathematician who formulated the laws of motion and gravitation.

entries.name: Charles Darwin
entries.desc: English naturalist whose theory of evolution by natural selection transformed biology.

entries.name: Florence Nightingale
entries.desc: Founder of modern nursing and a pioneer of statistics in public health.

entries.name: Marie Curie
entries.desc: Physicist and chemist, pioneer of radioactivity and the first person to win two Nobel Prizes.

entries.name: Mahatma Gandhi
entries.desc: Leader of India's nonviolent independence movement against British rule.

entries.name: Zhengde Emperor
entries.desc: 11th emperor of the Ming dynasty (1505–1521), known for unconventional rule and fondness for military pursuits.

entries.name: Longqing Emperor
entries.desc: 13th emperor of the Ming dynasty (1567–1572), whose brief reign brought pragmatic diplomatic and trade reforms.

entries.name: Wanli Emperor
entries.desc: 14th and longest-reigning Ming emperor (1572–1620), whose 48-year reign ended in prolonged political withdrawal.

entries.name: Taichang Emperor
entries.desc: 15th Ming emperor whose reign lasted less than a month in 1620 before his sudden and mysterious death.

entries.name: Tianqi Emperor
entries.desc: 16th Ming emperor (1620–1627) who ceded court power to the eunuch Wei Zhongxian while pursuing carpentry.

entries.name: Chongzhen Emperor
entries.desc: Last emperor of the Ming dynasty (1627–1644), who hanged himself as rebel forces captured Beijing.

entries.name: Tipu Sultan
entries.desc: Sultan of Mysore (1782–1799) who pioneered iron-cased rocket artillery and fiercely resisted British expansion.

entries.name: Baháʼu'lláh
entries.desc: Iranian founder of the Baháʼí Faith who proclaimed a new divine revelation in 1863 after decades of exile.

entries.name: Innocent X
entries.desc: Pope (1644–1655) who condemned the Peace of Westphalia and was immortalized by Velázquez's portrait.

entries.name: Soter
entries.desc: 2nd-century Pope of Rome who strengthened ties between the Roman church and Greek Christian communities.

entries.name: Innocent XI
entries.desc: Pope (1676–1689) who led the Holy League against Ottoman expansion and championed Church reform.

entries.name: Alexander VIII
entries.desc: Pope (1689–1691) known for nepotism and his condemnation of Jansenism during his brief pontificate.

entries.name: Suetonius
entries.desc: Roman historian of the early Empire, best known for The Twelve Caesars, biographies of Rome's first rulers.

entries.name: Henry I of England
entries.desc: Fourth son of William the Conqueror who seized the English throne in 1100 and stabilised the realm.

entries.name: Paul IV
entries.desc: Pope (1555–1559) who established the first Roman ghetto and intensified the Roman Inquisition.

entries.name: Pius II
entries.desc: Pope (1458–1464) and humanist scholar who called a failed crusade against the Ottoman Empire.

entries.name: Sergei Prokudin-Gorskii
entries.desc: Russian chemist and photographer who pioneered colour photography and documented early 20th-century Russia.

entries.name: Mary Wollstonecraft
entries.desc: English writer and philosopher whose A Vindication of the Rights of Woman (1792) is a founding text of feminism.

entries.name: Gaetano Donizetti
entries.desc: Italian composer of over 70 operas, a leading figure of the bel canto style alongside Rossini and Bellini.
